(**LBS**) is a [business school](/wiki/Business_school "Business school") and a constituent college of the [University of London](/wiki/University_of_London "University of London"). LBS was founded in 1964 and awards post-graduate degrees ([Master's degrees](/wiki/Master%27s_degree "Master's degree") in [management](/wiki/Management "Management") and [finance](/wiki/Finance "Finance"), [MBA](/wiki/Master_of_Business_Administration "Master of Business Administration") and [PhD](/wiki/PhD_in_management "PhD in management")). Its motto is "To have a profound impact on the way the world does business".[[4]](#cite_note-4)

**London Business School** (**LBS**) is a [business school](/wiki/Business_school "Business school") and a constituent college of the [University of London](/wiki/University_of_London "University of London"). LBS was founded in 1964 and awards post-graduate degrees ([Master's degrees](/wiki/Master%27s_degree "Master's degree") in [management](/wiki/Management "Management") and [finance](/wiki/Finance "Finance"), [MBA](/wiki/Master_of_Business_Administration "Master of Business Administration") and [PhD](/wiki/PhD_in_management "PhD in management")). Its motto is "To have a profound impact on the way the world does business".[[4]](#cite_note-4)

London Business School's main campus is located at [Sussex Place](/wiki/Sussex_Place "Sussex Place") in London, adjacent to [Regent's Park](/wiki/Regent%27s_Park "Regent's Park"). The school is located next to a social hub of restaurants and bars. In 2012, it expanded its teaching facilities by 70% by acquiring the [Marylebone Town Hall](/wiki/Marylebone_Town_Hall "Marylebone Town Hall") (now The [Sammy Ofer](/wiki/Sammy_Ofer "Sammy Ofer") Centre), and in 2017 the neighboring [Royal College of Obstetricians and Gynaecologists](/wiki/Royal_College_of_Obstetricians_and_Gynaecologists "Royal College of Obstetricians and Gynaecologists").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] LBS has a secondary campus in [Dubai](/wiki/Dubai "Dubai") that is dedicated to the Dubai EMBA and Executive Education.[[5]](#cite_note-:0-5)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=London_Business_School&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

### Foundation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=London_Business_School&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Foundation")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c6/LBS_campus.jpg/250px-LBS_campus.jpg)](/wiki/File:LBS_campus.jpg)

Sussex Place, main campus of the London Business School in London

London Business School was founded in 1964 under the name of the '*London Graduate School of Business Studies',* with Dr. Arthur Earle as Dean. In 1965, the school was registered as a company and was designated by the [University of London](/wiki/University_of_London "University of London") as an institution having recognised teachers. In 1966, the first Executive Development Programme was launched, followed by the Senior Executive Programme. The same year, a full-time MSc degree was also launched, with Sheila Cross enrolling as the School's first female student. In 1968, the School inaugurated the Sloan Fellowship MSc programme, which was the first one outside of the US. 17 students were enrolled and the programme was funded by the Alfred P. Sloan foundation. The first doctoral programme was established in 1969 and in 1970 Her Majesty [Queen Elizabeth II](/wiki/Elizabeth_II "Elizabeth II") opened the School's [Regent's Park](/wiki/Regent%27s_Park "Regent's Park") campus. The first PhD was awarded in 1974, and that year women made up over 15% of the student body for the first time. In 1983, the first part-time MBA programme was held under the direction of Sir Andrew Likierman, a former Dean of the School. In 1986, the school officially became the *London Business School* and was incorporated by [Royal Charter](/wiki/Royal_charter "Royal charter"), which gave LBS the right to confer and grant degrees. In 1992, the School was given the Queen's Award for Export in recognition of providing educational services to managers and companies worldwide. The following year, the school started its first Masters in Finance programme and in 2001, the EMBA-Global degree programme in partnership with the [Columbia Business School](/wiki/Columbia_Business_School "Columbia Business School").[[6]](#cite_note-6)

In 2007 a new campus was opened in [Dubai](/wiki/Dubai "Dubai") to offer both Executive MBA and Executive Education Programmes. In 2009, the school started two new programmes: The EMBA-Global Asia, in partnership with the [University of Hong Kong](/wiki/University_of_Hong_Kong "University of Hong Kong") and [Columbia Business School](/wiki/Columbia_Business_School "Columbia Business School"), and the Masters in Management (MiM). In 2012, the school acquired [Marylebone Town Hall](/wiki/Marylebone_Town_Hall "Marylebone Town Hall") and restored it with the objective of expanding its teaching facilities by 70 per cent.[[7]](#cite_note-LBS_Expansion-7) The building was renamed "The Sammy Ofer Centre" in honour of a generous donor, the Ofer Family, who made a gift for the development of the building, which was opened in 2017.[[8]](#cite_note-8) In 2016, LBS launched a new programme, the Masters in Financial Analysis, aimed at recent graduates who wish to pursue a career in finance, and acquired the lease of the neighbouring building of the Royal College of Obstetricians and Gynaenocologists, which it will occupy in 2020.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/7e/Honorary-Fellow-of-London-Business-School-03-july-2008---2.jpg/250px-Honorary-Fellow-of-London-Business-School-03-july-2008---2.jpg)](/wiki/File:Honorary-Fellow-of-London-Business-School-03-july-2008---2.jpg)

Group photo from the 2008 Honorary Fellowship Ceremony at London Business School, featuring Dean Professor Sir Andrew Likierman. Dean of London Business School at the time.

With the objective of increasing its size, the school organised a £100 million funding campaign. By the beginning of 2016, it had raised £98 million, £40 million of which will be used to renovate the Marylebone Town Hall, with £28 million for research, £18 million in scholarships for students, £10 million to increase the school's endowment, and £4 million to improve technology across the school.[[9]](#cite_note-9) By June 2016, the school had raised £125 million, including two £25 million gifts from alumni [Jim Ratcliffe](/wiki/Jim_Ratcliffe "Jim Ratcliffe") and [Idan Ofer](/wiki/Idan_Ofer "Idan Ofer").[[10]](#cite_note-ftraiseslbsmoules-10)

[François Ortalo-Magné](/wiki/Fran%C3%A7ois_Ortalo-Magn%C3%A9 "François Ortalo-Magné"), the French-born former Dean of the [Wisconsin School of Business](/wiki/Wisconsin_School_of_Business "Wisconsin School of Business"), succeeded Sir Andrew Likierman as Dean in August 2017.[[11]](#cite_note-ftlbsappoints-11) In January 2024, it was announced[[12]](#cite_note-12) that the Russian economist [Sergei Guriev](/wiki/Sergey_Guriyev "Sergey Guriyev"), previously the provost of the Institut d’études politiques in Paris ([Sciences Po](/wiki/Sciences_Po "Sciences Po")) would take over from Ortalo-Magné at the beginning of the 2024/2025 academic year, also joining the university's faculty[[13]](#cite_note-13) as a professor of economics. Guriev took up his post on 1 August 2024.[[14]](#cite_note-14)

## Academics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=London_Business_School&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Academics")]

### Master in Business Administration (MBA)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=London_Business_School&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Master in Business Administration (MBA)")]

The school's flagship programme is its full-time 15–21-month [Master of Business Administration](/wiki/Master_of_Business_Administration "Master of Business Administration") degree. MBA students take a prescribed set of core courses then choose from roughly 70 different electives. Class size has been around 400 students in every annual cohort. These are broken into 5 streams of approximately 80 students who take all core courses together.

Beyond academics, the school puts an emphasis on personal and professional development including [leadership](/wiki/Leadership_development "Leadership development"), global awareness, and business skill building. These developments are facilitated via specialized workshops led by external consultants, students, and faculty. In addition to a range of elective courses at the London Business School, the school has partnerships with around 32 exchange schools around the world. Each academic year around 100 students spend a term at another leading business school.

The MBA Programme has one of the world's largest international exchange programmes.[[20]](#cite_note-22) Each year approximately 35 per cent of second-year MBAs spend a term abroad at one of over 30 partner schools, including [NYU Stern School of Business](/wiki/New_York_University_Stern_School_of_Business "New York University Stern School of Business"), [IESE Business School](/wiki/IESE_Business_School "IESE Business School"), [Booth School of Business](/wiki/University_of_Chicago_Booth_School_of_Business "University of Chicago Booth School of Business") of [The University of Chicago](/wiki/University_of_Chicago "University of Chicago"), [The Wharton School](/wiki/Wharton_School "Wharton School") of the [University of Pennsylvania](/wiki/University_of_Pennsylvania "University of Pennsylvania"), [UCLA Anderson School of Management](/wiki/UCLA_Anderson_School_of_Management "UCLA Anderson School of Management"), the [MIT Sloan School of Management](/wiki/MIT_Sloan_School_of_Management "MIT Sloan School of Management"), [Tuck School of Business](/wiki/Tuck_School_of_Business "Tuck School of Business") at [Dartmouth College](/wiki/Dartmouth_College "Dartmouth College"), [Columbia Business School](/wiki/Columbia_Business_School "Columbia Business School"), [Kellogg School of Management](/wiki/Kellogg_School_of_Management "Kellogg School of Management") at [Northwestern University](/wiki/Northwestern_University "Northwestern University"), [Haas School of Business](/wiki/Haas_School_of_Business "Haas School of Business") at [UC Berkeley](/wiki/University_of_California,_Berkeley "University of California, Berkeley"), [McDonough School of Business](/wiki/McDonough_School_of_Business "McDonough School of Business") at [Georgetown University](/wiki/Georgetown_University "Georgetown University"), [Indian School of Business](/wiki/Indian_School_of_Business "Indian School of Business") among others.

### Executive MBA

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=London_Business_School&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Executive MBA")]

The school offers four Executive (part-time) MBA degrees, which are completed in 16–20 months. At an academic level, the school offers the same degree to both Executive (part-time) and full-time MBA students. The programmes involve very similar core courses to the full-time MBA, international field work and a wide range of elective courses. The course ends with a capstone together with company project or management report.

* **Executive MBA (London)**.
* **Executive MBA (Dubai)**. The programme begins with an orientation week in London. Following this, students take 10 core modules, which are taught in a four- or five-day block each month in Dubai. Students then undertake electives, which are primarily offered in London, and an international assignment. Two additional core modules take place in London.
* **EMBA-Global Americas and Europe**. A further 140 executives are enroled in the dual-degree EMBA-Global Programme. It is taught in partnership with [Columbia Business School](/wiki/Columbia_Business_School "Columbia Business School"). Graduates are awarded degrees from both universities. The first year involves week-long modules each month alternating between London and [New York](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City"). In the second year, students select from the full range of electives available at the participating schools.
* **EMBA-Global Asia**. launched in 2008 jointly with [Hong Kong University](/wiki/Hong_Kong_University "Hong Kong University") and Columbia. Teaching takes place at all three business schools. While the first year is modelled on the transatlantic EMBA-Global, the school states that because "EMBA-Global Asia is designed for people who have or will have significant trans-national responsibilities, all courses reflect a greater proportion of global material".[[21]](#cite_note-23)

### Masters in Finance

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=London_Business_School&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Masters in Finance")]

The school offers a [Master's in Finance](/wiki/Master%27s_in_Finance "Master's in Finance") ("MiF") programme on both a part- and full-time basis. Around 120 students attend the full-time programme, while 60 attend the part-time degree.

### Masters in Financial Analysis (MFA)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=London_Business_School&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Masters in Financial Analysis (MFA)")]

The Masters in Financial Analysis is the most recent programme offered by the London Business School, starting in September 2016 and consists of 12 months of courses. The programme targets recent graduates with less than a year of work experience who plan to start a career in finance, typically as an [analyst](/wiki/Financial_analyst "Financial analyst") in an investment bank or in [consulting](/wiki/Management_consulting "Management consulting"). The curriculum consists of 12 core courses based on 5 pillars ([Accounting](/wiki/Accounting "Accounting"), [Corporate Finance](/wiki/Corporate_finance "Corporate finance"), [Asset Management](/wiki/Asset_management "Asset management"), Financial Markets, and [Financial Econometrics](/wiki/Financial_econometrics "Financial econometrics")). The 12 courses are:[[22]](#cite_note-24)

* Corporate Finance
* Capital Structure
* Mergers & Acquisitions
* Investment Fundamentals
* Asset Management
* Analysis of Financial Statements
* Securities Valuation and Financial Modelling
* World Economy
* Financial Institutions
* Personal Finance
* Private Equity
* Data and Time Series Analytics

Students must also complete three electives of which a minimum of two must be related to finance.[[23]](#cite_note-25) The programme includes a business immersion week within a company ([Google](/wiki/Google "Google"), [Deloitte](/wiki/Deloitte "Deloitte"), [CNN](/wiki/CNN "CNN"), [Accenture](/wiki/Accenture "Accenture"), [Blackrock](/wiki/BlackRock "BlackRock") etc.) to work on case studies.[[24]](#cite_note-26) Finally, students participate in a Field Trip (study trip) that lasts a week. This travel experience consists of many networking dinners, company visits, and company presentations. The following destinations are available: [Silicon Valley](/wiki/Silicon_Valley "Silicon Valley"), [Paris](/wiki/Paris "Paris"), [Milan](/wiki/Milan "Milan") and [Munich](/wiki/Munich "Munich"), [Mumbai](/wiki/Mumbai "Mumbai") and [Bangalore](/wiki/Bangalore "Bangalore"), or [Shanghai](/wiki/Shanghai "Shanghai").[[25]](#cite_note-27)

### Masters in Management (MiM)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=London_Business_School&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Masters in Management (MiM)")]

The Masters in Management (MiM) is a one-year [master's degree](/wiki/Master%27s_degree "Master's degree") in management aimed at recent graduates who have less than one year of full-time postgraduate corporate work experience or less than two years of experience in a non-traditional business role.

The programme is structured in 3 terms, composed of the following core courses:[[26]](#cite_note-28)

First term:

* Financial Accounting
* Data Analytics for Management
* Finance
* Performance in Organisations

Second term:

* The Global Macroeconomy
* Marketing
* Strategic Analysis
* Decision and Risk Analysis

Third term:

* Applied Microeconomics
* Introduction to Management Accounting

Students must also follow 2 electives and can choose among 30 different courses.[[27]](#cite_note-29) The programme includes a business immersion week within a company ([Google](/wiki/Google "Google"), [Deloitte](/wiki/Deloitte "Deloitte"), [CNN](/wiki/CNN "CNN"), [Accenture](/wiki/Accenture "Accenture"), [Blackrock](/wiki/BlackRock "BlackRock") etc.) to work on case studies.[[28]](#cite_note-30) Finally, students have to participate in a Field Trip (study trip) that lasts a week. This travel experience consists of many networking dinners, company visits, and company presentations. The following destinations are available: [Silicon Valley](/wiki/Silicon_Valley "Silicon Valley"), [Paris](/wiki/Paris "Paris"), [Milan](/wiki/Milan "Milan") and [Munich](/wiki/Munich "Munich"), [Mumbai](/wiki/Mumbai "Mumbai") and [Bangalore](/wiki/Bangalore "Bangalore"), or [Shanghai](/wiki/Shanghai "Shanghai").[[29]](#cite_note-31).

## Research

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=London_Business_School&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Research")]

The school's 150 faculty work through 16 research centres or institutes.[[34]](#cite_note-36) According to the 2021 [Research Excellence Framework](/wiki/Research_Excellence_Framework "Research Excellence Framework"), the school came as the third department in the UK for [business and management research](/wiki/Business_and_management_research "Business and management research").[[35]](#cite_note-37)

### PhD programme

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=London_Business_School&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: PhD programme")]

The school offers a 5-year full-time [PhD](/wiki/Doctor_of_Philosophy "Doctor of Philosophy") programme. It supports 60 fully funded PhD candidates in seven doctoral programmes: Accounting, Economics, Finance, Management Science & Operations, Marketing, Organisational Behaviour, and Strategic & International Management.[[36]](#cite_note-38)

### Alumni associations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=London_Business_School&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Alumni associations")]

The London Business School has 50,000 alumni in more than 150 countries. Many local clubs (Paris, New-York, Zurich, etc.) organise recurrent events in their city.[[61]](#cite_note-63)
